message:
fix(widgets): continued mouse/scroll refinements, E2E test expansion, snapshot updates

- list/scrollbar/table/tree: refine mouse event handling edge cases
  and scroll clamping behavior
- simulator.rs: expand headless event injection capabilities
- mega_showcase_e2e.rs: expand E2E test coverage for showcase screens
- unicode_width_corpus.rs: additional VS16 edge cases
- Update help overlay and intrinsic sizing snapshots
- Sync beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1u5","title":"Document Inline vs Alt-Screen mode trade-offs","description":"# Document Inline vs Alt-Screen Mode Trade-offs

## Background & Strategic Importance
The plan requires documentation that explains "inline vs alt-screen explanation (scrollback tradeoffs)". This is critical for users to understand which mode to use for their application. The wrong choice leads to frustrated users who either:
- Lose their log history (alt-screen for an agent harness)
- Get cursor corruption (inline without understanding constraints)

## Why This Documentation Is Critical
- ADR-001 (Inline Mode Strategy) is a cornerstone decision
- Users coming from other TUI frameworks assume alt-screen
- Agent harness users NEED inline mode but don't know it exists
- Common source of "why doesn't my app work" questions

---

## Content Structure

### What Is Inline Mode?

**Definition:** The UI renders in the normal terminal buffer, interleaved with the terminal's scrollback history.

**How it works:**
```
[Previous terminal output]
[Previous terminal output]
[Previous terminal output]
[Log line 1]                 <- These scroll up into scrollback
[Log line 2]
[Log line 3]
├──────────────────────────┤
│ Status: Running tool... │ <- This is the pinned UI region
│ > Enter command: _      │    (fixed height, redrawn in place)
├──────────────────────────┤
```

**Key behaviors:**
- Scrollback is preserved (native terminal feature)
- Logs become part of terminal history
- Can scroll back after program exits to see full history
- UI occupies a fixed region at the bottom
- Default mode for ftui

**Technical implementation:**
- Never issue full-screen clear (`\x1b[2J`)
- Only redraw the bounded UI region
- Use EL (erase line) for local clearing
- Cursor must be restored after every present()

---

### What Is Alt-Screen Mode?

**Definition:** The UI renders in the alternate screen buffer, a separate buffer from the primary terminal.

**How it works:**
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                      │
│         Full-screen TUI              │
│                                      │
│    No scrollback in this buffer      │
│                                      │
│                                      │
│   Status bar at bottom               │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key behaviors:**
- Scrollback is NOT preserved (alt buffer is blank)
- When program exits, previous content is restored
- Classic TUI experience (vim, htop, less)
- Full-screen clears are safe
- Opt-in via `ScreenMode::AltScreen`

**Technical implementation:**
- Enter: `\x1b[?1049h` (smcup)
- Exit: `\x1b[?1049l` (rmcup)
- Previous screen automatically restored on exit

---

### Trade-off Matrix

| Feature | Inline Mode | Alt-Screen Mode |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| Scrollback preserved | ✓ | ✗ |
| Full-screen clear safe | ✗ | ✓ |
| Log history after exit | ✓ | ✗ |
| Cursor management | Complex | Simple |
| Classic TUI feel | ✗ | ✓ |
| Agent harness ideal | ✓ | ✗ |
| Resize handling | Requires anchor | Straightforward |
| Panic cleanup | Critical | Important |

---

### When to Use Inline Mode

**Use inline mode when:**
- Building agent harness UIs (Claude Code, Codex style)
- Log viewers where history matters after exit
- REPLs and interactive shells
- Any app where scrollback is valuable
- Applications that stream output the user may want to review

**Examples:**
- Claude Code's tool execution output
- `npm install` progress that you want to scroll back through
- Interactive database REPL with query history
- Build tool output (cargo, make)

**Configuration:**
```rust
App::new(model)
    .screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 4 })
    .run()
```

---

### When to Use Alt-Screen Mode

**Use alt-screen mode when:**
- Building full-screen dashboards (htop, btop)
- Traditional TUI applications (vim, nano)
- Modal dialogs and pickers that need full screen
- When you WANT content gone on exit (games, temporary views)
- Visual applications where scrollback is meaningless

**Examples:**
- System monitor dashboard
- Text editor
- File manager
- Interactive game
- Configuration wizard

**Configuration:**
```rust
App::new(model)
    .screen_mode(ScreenMode::AltScreen)
    .run()
```

---

### Mixed Strategy (Best of Both Worlds)

The plan supports a hybrid approach: Start in inline mode, temporarily switch to alt-screen for complex modals, then return to inline mode.

**When to use mixed mode:**
- Agent harness with occasional file picker
- REPL that needs an interactive editor
- Log viewer that spawns a detail modal

**Implementation:**
```rust
// Start in inline mode
App::new(model)
    .screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline {
        ui_height: 6,
    })
    .run()

// In update(), when needing full-screen modal:
fn update(&mut self, msg: Msg) -> Cmd<Msg> {
    match msg {
        Msg::OpenFilePicker => {
            self.mode = Mode::FilePicker;
            Cmd::enter_alt_screen()
        }
        Msg::FilePickerClosed(path) => {
            self.mode = Mode::Normal;
            Cmd::batch(vec![
                Cmd::exit_alt_screen(),
                Cmd::msg(Msg::FileSelected(path)),
            ])
        }
        // ...
    }
}
```

**What happens:**
1. App runs in inline mode (scrollback preserved)
2. User triggers file picker
3. Switch to alt-screen (full-screen picker UI)
4. User makes selection
5. Switch back to inline mode
6. Scrollback still intact!

---

### Implementation Details

#### Inline Mode Contract

1. **Never clear full screen**
   - `\x1b[2J` destroys scrollback
   - Only clear the UI region

2. **Only clear bounded UI region**
   - Calculate UI region from bottom
   - Use EL (erase line) for clearing

3. **Cursor restored after every present()**
   - Frame::cursor_position specifies target
   - Presenter handles restoration

4. **Logs enter scrollback naturally**
   - Print log lines above UI region
   - Terminal scrolls them into history

#### Alt-Screen Contract

1. **Full-screen clears allowed**
   - Safe because alt buffer is isolated

2. **Simpler cursor policy**
   - No anchor calculation needed

3. **Exit restores previous content**
   - rmcup sequence handles this

4. **Cleanup on panic still required**
   - Must restore primary screen
   - RAII guard handles this

---

### Common Mistakes

#### Mistake 1: Full-screen clear in inline mode

```rust
// WRONG: Destroys scrollback!
write!(stdout, "\x1b[2J")?;

// WRONG: Also destroys scrollback!
frame.buffer.clear();  // If this issues ED 2

// RIGHT: Clear only the UI region
for y in ui_start..ui_end {
    frame.buffer.set_string(0, y, &" ".repeat(width), Style::default());
}
```

**Why it's wrong:** ESC[2J erases the entire screen, which in inline mode means destroying the scrollback history that makes inline mode valuable.

#### Mistake 2: Assuming scrollback in alt-screen

```rust
// WRONG assumption: "The user can scroll back to see logs"
// In alt-screen, there IS NO scrollback!
app.screen_mode(ScreenMode::AltScreen)
    .run()
// Logs are gone when app exits

// RIGHT: Use inline mode for log-heavy apps
app.screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 4 })
    .run()
// Logs preserved in scrollback
```

**Why it's wrong:** Alt-screen uses a separate buffer that has no scrollback. When the app exits, everything is gone.

#### Mistake 3: Not handling resize in inline mode

```rust
// WRONG: Fixed anchor that doesn't update
let anchor = 5;  // Assume UI starts at line 5

// RIGHT: Recalculate anchor on resize
fn on_resize(&mut self, size: Size) {
    self.anchor = size.height - self.ui_height;
}
```

**Why it's wrong:** In inline mode, the UI region anchor is relative to the bottom. When the terminal resizes, the anchor must be recalculated.

#### Mistake 4: Not testing with PTY tests

```rust
// Easy to miss cursor corruption in manual testing
// PTY tests catch it automatically

#[test]
fn test_inline_mode_cursor_contract() {
    let mut term = TerminalModel::new(80, 24);
    let app = TestApp::new();

    app.render(&mut term);

    // Cursor should be at input line, not corrupted
    assert_eq!(term.cursor_position(), (2, 23));
}
```

---

### Debugging Screen Mode Issues

#### Symptom: Scrollback lost on exit

**Cause:** Using alt-screen when inline mode was intended.
**Fix:** Change to `ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: N }`.

#### Symptom: Cursor jumps around

**Cause:** Writing to terminal outside present() cycle.
**Fix:** Ensure all output goes through ftui runtime.

#### Symptom: UI region flickers

**Cause:** Clearing too much on each frame.
**Fix:** Only redraw changed cells (presenter handles this).

#### Symptom: Logs overwrite UI

**Cause:** UI height not properly configured.
**Fix:** Set `ui_height` to match your actual UI region height.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both modes clearly explained with diagrams
- [ ] Trade-off matrix complete and accurate
- [ ] Use cases documented with examples
- [ ] Mixed strategy explained with code
- [ ] Common mistakes listed with WRONG/RIGHT examples
- [ ] Debugging section for troubleshooting
- [ ] Links to related ADR (ADR-001)
- [ ] Code examples compile

---

 {"id":"bd-1un","title":"Document Terminal Compatibility Matrix","description":"# Terminal Compatibility Matrix Documentation

## Background
Plan Chapter 16 provides a detailed compatibility matrix. This needs to be documented for users to understand what features work where.

## Compatibility Matrix (from Plan)

| Feature | Kitty | WezTerm | Alacritty | Ghostty | iTerm2 | GNOME Term | Win Term |
|---------|-------|---------|-----------|---------|--------|------------|----------|
| True Color | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Sync Output | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| Kitty Keyboard | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Kitty Graphics | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Sixel | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Focus Events | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Bracketed Paste | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

## Feature Details

### True Color (24-bit RGB)
- Detection: COLORTERM=truecolor or COLORTERM=24bit
- Fallback: 256-color or 16-color palette
- Most modern terminals support this

### Synchronized Output (DEC 2026)
- Purpose: Atomic frame updates, flicker prevention
- Detection: Known terminal identification
- Fallback: Buffered writes still help
- ftui activates when detected

### OSC 8 Hyperlinks
- Purpose: Clickable links in terminal
- Format: `\x1b]8;;URL\x1b\\text\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`
- Windows Terminal: Not supported
- Most Linux/macOS terminals support

### Kitty Keyboard Protocol
- Purpose: Enhanced key event information
- Provides: press/repeat/release, modifiers
- Limited adoption outside Kitty ecosystem
- Feature-gated in ftui

### Scroll Region (DECSTBM)
- Purpose: Inline mode optimization
- Support: Nearly universal
- Quirks: tmux/screen passthrough issues
- ftui uses as optimization, not requirement

## Multiplexer Notes

### tmux
- Passthrough: Requires `\x1bPtmux;...\x1b\\`
- Sync output: Blocked by default
- OSC 8: Needs recent version + config
- Detection: TMUX env var

### screen
- Limited modern feature support
- No sync output passthrough
- Basic ANSI works fine
- Detection: STY env var

### Zellij
- Better passthrough than tmux/screen
- Native terminal support varies
- Detection: ZELLIJ env var

## Windows Considerations

### Windows Terminal
- True color: ✓
- Mouse: ✓
- OSC 8: ✗
- Sync output: ✗
- Raw mode: Via Crossterm

### ConEmu/Cmder
- Limited feature set
- True color: ✓ (newer versions)
- ANSI support varies by version

### cmd.exe / PowerShell (legacy)
- Very limited ANSI support
- Windows 10+ improved support
- Use Windows Terminal for best experience

## Inline Mode Notes
- Works everywhere (no special requirements)
- Cursor save/restore: Universal
- Scroll region: Nearly universal (optimization only)
- Sync output: Nice-to-have (not required)

## Recommended Test Matrix
For CI, test against:
1. Kitty (most features)
2. WezTerm (cross-platform)
3. Alacritty (popular, minimal)
4. Plain Linux VT (baseline)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Matrix table documented
- [ ] Feature descriptions complete
- [ ] Multiplexer notes included
- [ ] Windows section clear
- [ ] Inline mode notes present
- [ ] Test matrix recommended

Cover diagram layout/render basics and error handling.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Deterministic unit tests cover diagram parsing/rendering primitives and edge cases (invalid input).\n2) Assertions/logs include input source and expected vs actual output/hashes.\n3) No mocks/fakes; use real diagram fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:15:35.615881118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T22:56:56.540523554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T22:56:56.540505540Z","close_reason":"Added diagram.rs unit tests for coverage (feature diagram).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1vb18","title":"Fix emoji-triggered border corruption in demo showcase (keep emojis)","description":"Problem
- Demo showcase has border misalignment/corruption in File Browser and Markdown screens when emojis are present. Emojis must remain.

Likely Causes
1) Several screens try to "clear" the full area using Paragraph::new("") + bg style, but Paragraph only paints style and does not clear cell content. With Flex gaps between panels, stale border characters from prior frames/screens can bleed into those gaps and appear as misaligned borders.
2) Buffer::print_text_clipped (ftui-render) advances by grapheme_width() but may only write the first char for multi-codepoint graphemes (e.g. VS16 sequences like "⚙️"), leaving holes that can preserve stale border chars.

Fix
- Clear demo screen root areas with actual space cells (bg=surface) so gaps are deterministically blank.
- Make Buffer::print_text_clipped fill any extra width when falling back to first char so no holes remain (and add a regression test).

Acceptance
- File Browser and Markdown screens render without stray/misaligned borders in gaps.
- Emojis remain enabled in file browser icons and unicode showcase.
- cargo fmt/check/clippy/test pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:01:09.704771751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:01:19.684772851Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["borders","demo-showcase","emoji","render","text"]}
+{"id":"bd-1vb18","title":"Fix emoji-triggered border corruption in demo showcase (keep emojis)","description":"Problem
- Demo showcase has border misalignment/corruption in File Browser and Markdown screens when emojis are present. Emojis must remain.

Likely Causes
1) Several screens try to "clear" the full area using Paragraph::new("") + bg style, but Paragraph only paints style and does not clear cell content. With Flex gaps between panels, stale border characters from prior frames/screens can bleed into those gaps and appear as misaligned borders.
2) Buffer::print_text_clipped (ftui-render) advances by grapheme_width() but may only write the first char for multi-codepoint graphemes (e.g. VS16 sequences like "⚙️"), leaving holes that can preserve stale border chars.

Fix
- Clear demo screen root areas with actual space cells (bg=surface) so gaps are deterministically blank.
- Make Buffer::print_text_clipped fill any extra width when falling back to first char so no holes remain (and add a regression test).

Acceptance
- File Browser and Markdown screens render without stray/misaligned borders in gaps.
- Emojis remain enabled in file browser icons and unicode showcase.
- cargo fmt/check/clippy/test pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:01:09.704771751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706728130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T02:34:21.706701631Z","close_reason":"Completed (emoji border corruption fixed: VS16 width policy + buffer filler + empty paragraph clears)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["borders","demo-showcase","emoji","render","text"]}

 {"id":"bd-1xhje","title":"Inline present cleanup on I/O error","description":"TerminalWriter::present_inline uses ? on writes; on IO error it returns before restoring cursor/sync/scroll-region, leaving the terminal in a bad state if the runtime keeps going. Add best-effort cleanup on error.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseBeacon","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:38.633291975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594195276Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594177763Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: present_inline wraps errors and calls best_effort_inline_cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1xo","title":"Windows Terminal compatibility layer","description":"PHASE 7 - POLISH: WINDOWS SUPPORT

## Background
Windows Terminal v1 support is deferred but must have clear integration path. Modern Windows Terminal supports ANSI, older conhost needs different handling.

## Technical Requirements (v1 Scope - Windows Terminal Only)
- Detect Windows Terminal vs legacy conhost
- Enable VT processing mode via SetConsoleMode
- Handle Windows-specific escape sequences
- PTY spawning via ConPTY API
- Signal handling differences (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+Break)
- Path handling for Windows conventions

## Out of Scope for v1
- Legacy conhost support (Windows 7/8)
- PowerShell-specific handling
- Windows-specific widgets

## Design Rationale
Per ADR-004, Windows v1 targets modern Windows Terminal only. This dramatically simplifies implementation while covering majority of Windows developer use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Windows Terminal detection
- [ ] VT mode enabling
- [ ] ConPTY integration for PTY tests
- [ ] Signal handling shim
- [ ] CI testing on Windows
- [ ] Feature gate: windows

## Testing
- GitHub Actions Windows runner
- Manual testing on Windows Terminal
- Document known limitations
